title: Homogenate of A. cervicornis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"442pb","text":"This protocol is to study disease in Acropora cervicornis by using a disease homogenate method. These methods are adapted from methods presented in Muller 2018 https://elifesciences.org/articles/35066.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":160,"length":40}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"2E267B09102F11EB9C180A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://elifesciences.org/articles/35066"}}}}

steps: Clean -up
Bleach tweezers, clippers, slates, airgun, surfaces
Dose experimental corals with the placebo and disease homogenates

Obtain healthy and diseased Acropora cervicornis fragments. These fragments are preferably collected from the same area and with similar disease progression. The tissue areas should be similar between healthy and disease corals to prepare diseased and placebo blastates with similar concentrations.
Prepare placebo and disease homogenates
Grab healthy fragments from the holding tank. Sigle branch fragments are easier to blast and to estimate their tissue area. If cutting different branches, use clean clippers.

Grab a fragment and hold it over a pre-labeled Ziploc bag (** this will require two people**). Airbrush the tissue with an airgun and FSW. Approximately 00:05:00, per fragment Number of fragments to blast Work in a fume hood if possible to avoid blasting aerosols. When working with disease fragments, remove the tissue up to 5 cm above the lesion.
Take a picture of every fragment with a size scale to estimate the amount of tissue blasted.
Pour homogenate in a pre-labeled 50mL falcon tube(s). Measure volume and store in the fridge.
Grab diseased fragments from the holding tank. And repeat steps 2.2 - 2.5.
Bring placebo and diseased homogenates to the same volume by adding FSW
Add 20 beads to each falcon tube containing ~ 40mL of homogenate and vortex for 10 minutes 00:10:00, each tube
Sterile razor with bleach and scratch a small area of each experimental coral Create a ring around the coral located ~ 1cm from the bottom
Apply 500 uL of placebo and disease homogenate above each coral fragments in their respective treatments.
Save and preserve ~ 500 ul of both homogenates for sequencing.
Change gloves
Add 16 L of seawater to each tank to completely cover the fragments
Discard bags and falcon tubes
Rinse fragments with FSW, and place them in a sterile container with enough FSW to cover them.
Maintained closed system for 3hours
creator: Ana M Palacio-Castro
